title: Tragacantha schahrudensis
taxonomy: Tragacantha schahrudensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to Iran and is a type of legume. The plant is further classified as a subgenus within the genus Astragalus, which is a large and diverse group of plants. Tragacantha schahrudensis is a perennial herb with a taproot system and produces small, white or pink flowers. This plant is also known as a milkvetch due to its ability to produce a milky sap when injured.

It is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
